tests.name: Cancel button exists hidden in DOM
tests.notes: getElementById('chat-cancel-btn') found. Has class 'hidden'. Text: '✕ Cancel'. chat-send-btn also present. Verified via browser evaluate.

tests.name: POST /api/build/cancel returns { success: true }
tests.notes: Browser fetch('/api/build/cancel', {method:'POST'}) returned status 200, body {success:true, was_in_progress:false}. CSRF origin check passes from same-origin browser context.

tests.name: Build lock clears on server restart (BUILD_LOCK_STALE)
tests.notes: Set build_in_progress:true in .studio.json. Killed and restarted server with SITE_TAG=site-auntie-gale-garage-sales. Server log showed '[studio] BUILD_LOCK_STALE — cleared on startup'. .studio.json confirmed build_in_progress:false after restart.

tests.name: Second build request rejected cleanly when build is in progress
tests.notes: Sent content_update WS message ('update the nav phone number'). Waited for 'Sending to Claude...' status. Sent second WS chat message during live Claude call. Server responded with type:'chat' content:'A build is already in progress. Please wait for it to finish before sending another request.' Guard fires in handleChatMessage() at line 6979. NOTE: layout_update/new_site messages classified before build executes go through handlePlanning() which has no build lock guard — double-plan can occur. Only post-approval builds are guarded.
tests.rejection_message: A build is already in progress. Please wait for it to finish before sending another request.

tests.name: Brief with technical vocabulary classifies as build with HIGH confidence
tests.notes: Sent: "I'm building a site for Test Co. PAGES NEEDED: Home, About. COLOR SCHEME: Primary #E8420A. DATA ATTRIBUTES: All sections need data-section-id, data-field-id." Server log: [classifier] intent=build confidence=HIGH signals=2 (strong build pattern). Two strong build signals matched: /\bpages\s+needed\b/ and /\bcolor\s+scheme\b.*\bfonts?\b/ (actually /pages needed/ + /color scheme/). Response was a build plan card — correct behavior.

tests.name: Inspection command classifies as visual_inspect (not build)
tests.notes: Original test phrase 'inspect the data-field-id on the hero section' misclassified as layout_update (LOW confidence) because 'data-field-id' is not in the visual_inspect noun list (nav|header|footer|hero|layout|section|etc). Re-tested with 'inspect the hero section data attributes' — correctly classified as visual_inspect. Server status: 'Inspecting site...' Response: file-based site inspection result (hero section, page summary). NO build plan generated. KNOWN GAP: 'inspect the data-field-id' phrasing not covered by classifier patterns.
tests.original_phrase: inspect the data-field-id on the hero section
tests.corrected_phrase: inspect the hero section data attributes
tests.corrected_result: visual_inspect — correct, returns inspection data not build plan
tests.gap: data-field-id not in visual_inspect noun allowlist at classifier line 4389

tests.name: Insert component via chat
tests.notes: Original test phrase 'use the display-stage component on the homepage' misclassified as layout_update (LOW confidence) because classifier pattern at line 4499 requires 'from/in the library' AND only allows one word before 'component' keyword. 'display-stage' is hyphenated two-word name that exceeds (?:\w+[\s-])? single-word capture. Re-tested with 'use the stage component from the library' — correctly classified as component_import. Component inserted successfully: index.html grew from 24002 to 26380 bytes (+2378). reload-preview event fired. Server confirmed: 'Inserted Display Stage — Deal Showcase into index.html. Used in: 1 site(s)'. KNOWN GAP: multi-word hyphenated component IDs (display-stage, photo-slideshow) cannot be inserted via natural language — single-word alias required.
tests.original_phrase: use the display-stage component on the homepage
tests.original_result: layout_update — generates build plan instead of direct insert
tests.working_phrase: use the stage component from the library
tests.working_result: component_import — direct insert without plan, index.html updated

tests.name: Library tab panel shows components
tests.notes: Clicked Library tab in right sidebar. Panel became visible. 6 components listed: Video Hero Section (video-hero), Garage Sale Product Card (product-card-garage), Display Stage — Deal Showcase (display-stage), CSS Starburst Badge (starburst-badge), Live Countdown Timer (countdown-timer), Category Filter Pills (category-filter-pills). Insert tip text shown: 'To insert: "use the [name] component on the homepage"'

tests.name: Chat lists all 6 components when asked
tests.notes: Sent: 'what components are in the library'. Classified as component_import. Status: 'Checking component library...'. Response: '**Component Library** (6 components):' followed by all 6 with descriptions and IDs. No build plan generated.
tests.response_preview: **Component Library** (6 components): Video Hero Section, Garage Sale Product Card, Display Stage...

known_gaps_found.description: component_import classifier pattern (?:\w+[\s-])? only matches single-word component names before 'component' keyword. Multi-word hyphenated IDs like 'display-stage', 'photo-slideshow' cannot be inserted by natural name.
known_gaps_found.workaround: Use single-word alias: 'use the stage component from the library' instead of 'use the display-stage component'

known_gaps_found.description: Visual inspect classifier at line 4389 does not include 'data-field-id', 'data-section-id', or other data attribute names in its noun list. 'inspect the data-field-id on the hero' misclassifies to layout_update.
known_gaps_found.workaround: Rephrase to 'inspect the hero section' instead of 'inspect the data-field-id'

known_gaps_found.description: handlePlanning() has no buildInProgress guard. Two concurrent plan-generating messages can both proceed through planning simultaneously. Guard only fires in handleChatMessage() after plan approval.
known_gaps_found.location: server.js line 5123 (handlePlanning has no guard vs line 6979 which does)
known_gaps_found.workaround: User must not approve two plans simultaneously

summary.notes: Both PARTIAL-PASSes are classifier routing issues with known workaround phrasings. Core features (cancel button, build lock stale clear, double-build rejection, library panel, component listing, component insert) all work correctly. The component insert and inspection tests pass with the correct phrasing.

screenshots.s1-03-classifier-inspect.png: Inspection result displayed — not a build plan
